questions.question: Consider a prisoner's dilemma game where two players, A and B, can choose between cooperating or defecting. In sub-game perfect equilibrium, player A only defects if player B defects. What is the expected outcome for player A in this scenario?
questions.hint: Recall the definition of sub-game perfect equilibrium and the payoffs in the prisoner's dilemma game.

questions.question: In a game of chicken, two drivers, A and B, drive towards each other at constant speeds. In sub-game perfect equilibrium, driver A will swerve at the exact moment before driver B. What is the likely outcome of this game?
questions.hint: Consider the payoffs and strategies involved in the game of chicken.

questions.question: Two firms, X and Y, have the same production costs and sell the same product. In sub-game perfect equilibrium, firm X will undercut firm Y's price. What is the expected outcome for firm Y in this scenario?
questions.hint: Recall the concept of price competition and the Nash equilibrium.
